what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim that replies to an earlier point about draft-queue durability, agreeing that drafts must persist locally and adding that the intended backend for the feature is Supabase realtime.
observations: YAML frontmatter marks this as a note of type 'note' authored by 'tim' in channel 'dragnet-development'
It has a 'commented_on' link to board://item-83ecc67b
Created and updated timestamps are identical, indicating a single filing
Body quotes a prior point about IndexedDB-based draft-queue persistence, iOS Safari PWA tab eviction, and the risk of in-memory queues
Tim agrees the point is valid and needed
Tim mentions Supabase realtime as the intended backend, while conceding it may not apply in all cases
standouts: Explicit agreement that draft queue durability via local persistence is required
Specific mention of iOS Safari PWA background eviction as a failure mode
Mention of Supabase realtime as intended backend, with an open caveat that it may not cover offline/backgrounded scenarios
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a markdown body with a quoted prior point followed by a short reply
concerns: The reply is vague about how Supabase realtime relates to offline/backgrounded persistence
No decision, action item, or implementation direction is recorded
The caveat 'maybe that doesn't apply all the time' leaves the design boundary unresolved
notes: Tim's reply on a durability point: agrees drafts must survive app-kill via local persistence, and notes Supabase realtime is the intended backend but may not cover every case.
questions: What is the content of the parent item board://item-83ecc67b?
What does 'all this' specifically refer to in the backend context?
How is Supabase realtime expected to interact with an offline IndexedDB draft queue?
Has a decision been made to require IndexedDB persistence for the draft queue?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file's stated purpose is a discussion note/comment, and it does record a reply that acknowledges a concern and adds backend context.
apparent_themes: PWA reliability under mobile OS lifecycle pressure
Realtime backend limitations versus offline/backgrounded scenarios
Avoiding credibility-eroding data loss in user-generated drafts
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to validate the durability concern and inject relevant backend context (Supabase realtime) while leaving room for the fact that realtime sync does not eliminate the need for local offline persistence.
novelty: It pairs an endorsement of IndexedDB draft-queue durability with a mention of a realtime backend, highlighting a potential mismatch between sync infrastructure and offline/backgrounded behavior.
contribution: Preserves an acknowledgment that draft queues must persist locally to survive app-kill and a note that Supabase realtime is the intended backend, which together form design context about the boundary between sync and offline persistence.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard reply by Tim agreeing that draft queues must persist locally to survive mobile PWA eviction and noting Supabase realtime as the intended backend.
intention_for_embedding: To support a durability requirement and add backend-context to the discussion of offline draft persistence.
